titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Collection of Bookplates by Dmitrij R. Saenko
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Sonja Foard

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday December 7, 2018 at 00:04

did.unittitle: Collection of bookplates by Dmitrij R Saenko
did.abstract: The collection includes examples of bookplates designed by Dmitrij R. Saenko, dating from 1994-2003.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has not been transferred to Yale University.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Collection of Bookplates by Dmitrij R. Saenko, Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library, Yale University.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Dmitrij Saenko (also spelled Dmitry Sayenko) is a contemporary Russian artist and proprietor of Nikodim-Press. He is active in the book arts, and he experimented with the bookplate form early in his career. Saenko works with different printmaking techniques as well as different styles of expression.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection includes examples of bookplates designed by Dmitrij R. Saenko. The specimens date from 1994 to 2003 and include both etchings and linocuts.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Bookplates, 1994-2003. II. Correspondence and Ephemera, 2010-2013.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by the patron's name. Names appearing in Cyrillic characters have been romanized and are indicated by brackets.

did.unittitle: Biblioteca Popular de Gral. San Martin
Print copy of PDF supplied by the artist (March 18, 2013)
